title: 10 best herbal creams for loss of appetite

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Adiantum capillus-veneris may help reduce inflammation in the body, which could potentially alleviate symptoms of anorexia or loss of appetite.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidant compounds present in Adiantum capillus-veneris may help protect the body from oxidative stress and damage, which could contribute to improved overall health and potentially increase appetite.

Stomach-soothing properties: Some traditional remedies suggest that Adiantum capillus-veneris can help soothe digestive issues like nausea, bloating, or stomach pain, which are common symptoms of loss of appetite.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba, also known as maidenhair tree, creams helps with loss of appetite because of its unique antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. The extract from the ginkgo biloba plant is believed to enhance blood flow and oxygenation to the brain, which may stimulate the appetite and improve digestion. Additionally, ginkgo biloba creams contain flavonoids and terpenoids that may help to regulate gut hormones, leading to an increase in appetite and improved overall digestive health.
remedy_constituents: Bilobalide: A triterpene that helps increase appetite by potentially improving gastric function and reducing inflammation in the digestive tract.
Quercetin: A flavonoid that may help stimulate appetite by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in the gut, thereby promoting a healthy digestive system.
Ginkgolide B: A sesquiterpene lactone that may aid in increasing appetite by potentially modulating the gut-brain axis and reducing symptoms of gastrointestinal disorders.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Extracts from Ginkgo biloba leaves are used in creams due to their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help with skin health.
Barks: Ginkgo biloba barks are sometimes used in creams for their potential antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help with skin health.
Seeds: Ginkgo biloba seeds are not commonly used in creams, but they have been traditionally used in some herbal remedies due to their potential antioxidant properties.
remedy_recipe: Infuse 20 grams of dried ginkgo biloba leaves in 200ml of carrier oil such as jojoba oil at 8 hours.
Combine 10 grams of beeswax with 20 grams of shea butter and 10 grams of cocoa butter in a double boiler.
Melt the wax and butter mixture at 120 degrees fahrenheit for 10 minutes then let it cool slightly.
Add the infused oil to the cooled wax and butter mixture then stir until fully incorporated at 5 minutes.
Pour the mixture into containers and let it cool and solidify completely at 30 minutes to 1 hour.

remedy_properties: Digestive soothing compounds: Peppermint cream contains digestive soothing compounds like menthol, menthone, and limonene, which can help to ease digestive discomfort and reduce nausea, making it easier to consume food and regain appetite.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory properties in peppermint cream may help to reduce inflammation in the gut, which can contribute to loss of appetite by alleviating symptoms like bloating, cramping, and pain.

Menthol's stimulation of digestion: Menthol in peppermint cream can stimulate digestive enzymes and increase gastric motility, helping to improve digestion and reduce feelings of fullness or discomfort that can lead to a decrease in appetite.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita, also known as peppermint, creams helps with loss of appetite because of its invigorating properties that stimulate digestion and improve nutrient absorption. The menthol in peppermint creams can increase saliva production and enhance the digestive enzymes, which helps to break down food efficiently. This can help restore appetite and interest in food, making it an effective remedy for individuals experiencing loss of appetite due to digestive issues or other underlying health conditions. Its refreshing aroma also contributes to its appetite-stimulating effects.
remedy_constituents: Menthol: Menthol acts as a digestive stimulant and helps increase appetite by stimulating the digestive system and improving gut motility.

Limonene (specifically beta-pinene and beta-myrcene): Limonene derivatives in peppermint may aid in increasing appetite by reducing anxiety and stress, which can be contributing factors to loss of appetite.

Rosmarinic acid: As a phenolic compound, rosmarinic acid may help stimulate appetite by exerting its antioxidant properties and reducing inflammation in the digestive system, thus promoting healthy gut function.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used for their aromatic and digestive properties, which help stimulate appetite and alleviate digestive issues.
Stems: Used for their peppermint oil content, which is known to relieve nausea and promote appetite.
Flowers: Used for their calming and carminative properties, which help soothe digestive issues and stimulate appetite.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh leaves of mentha x piperita at dawn or early morning when essential oils are most concentrated.
Steam distill the harvested leaves for 2 hours to produce 20-30 milliliters of mentha x piperita essential oil.
Mix 5 grams of mentha x piperita essential oil with 5 grams of beeswax and 10 grams of sweet almond oil.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler for 10-15 minutes or until the beeswax is fully incorporated.
Pour the mixture into small containers and allow it to cool and solidify at room temperature for 30 minutes.

remedy_properties: Mucilage content: Slippery elm cream contains a thick, protective mucilage that coats the mucous membranes in the mouth and throat, soothing irritation and inflammation associated with decreased appetite, making it more comfortable to eat and drink.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ulmus rubra, also known as slippery elm, creams helps with loss of appetite because of its soothing and demulcent properties. remedy_parts: Barks: They are used to make creams due to their potential anti-inflammatory properties, which might help stimulate appetite.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 30 grams of fresh ulmus rubra leaves and roots from a clean environment for their medicinal properties.
Dry the collected plant material in a single layer at 35 degrees celsius for 24 hours to remove moisture.
Combine 30 grams of dried ulmus rubra material with 90 grams of beeswax in a heat-resistant bowl to create the base.
Heat the mixture at 60 degrees celsius for 30 minutes and then stir in 60 grams of coconut oil to achieve the desired consistency.
Allow the mixture to cool and set at room temperature for 2 hours before transferring it to containers for storage.

Stems: Stems of Zingiber officinale may also be used in creams for loss of appetite due to their potential to enhance digestion and reduce nausea.
remedy_recipe: Extract 10 grams of dried zingiber officinale rhizome in 100ml of carrier oil for 2 weeks.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth and discard the solids after 2 weeks.
Mix 5 grams of beeswax with 10 grams of shea butter in a double boiler for 10 minutes.
Combine the oil extract with the melted wax and butter mixture in a 1:1 ratio.
Pour the mixture into glass jars and allow it to cool and set at room temperature for 30 minutes.

Choline: Choline, an alkaloid, plays a crucial role in the production of acetylcholine, a neurotransmitter that regulates appetite and digestion. Its presence in Fennel may help stimulate appetite by enhancing digestive function.
remedy_parts: Seeds: Used for their carminative and digestive properties, which help stimulate appetite.

remedy_recipe: Gather 250g of dried foeniculum vulgare flowers and 500ml of sweet almond oil in a clean environment.
Infuse the dried flowers in the sweet almond oil for 4 hours at room temperature under a lid.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean container to separate the solids from the liquid.
Combine 200g of beeswax and 100g of shea butter with the strained liquid in a double boiler at medium heat.
Stir the mixture continuously until it reaches a temperature of 50°c then let it cool and solidify completely.

remedy_recipe: Melt 200g of shea butter in a double boiler at 45-50 degrees celsius for 15 minutes.
Mix 100g of beeswax with 50g of coconut oil in a separate bowl.
Combine the melted shea butter with the beeswax and coconut oil mixture in a slow stir.
Add 100g of curcuma longa extract powder and 20ml of jojoba oil to the mixture.
Stir the mixture at 45-50 degrees celsius for 10 minutes and pour into containers.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of dried lavandula angustifolia flowers at their peak potency for optimal extraction.

Mix 20g of beeswax and 10g of emulsifying wax with 100g of the infused oil in a double boiler.
Allow the mixture to cool and thicken for 30 minutes before transferring it to a container.

Infuse the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 weeks shaking the jar every 2 days to extract the herbal properties.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth into a clean glass container and discard the solids then add 2% beeswax to create a smooth cream texture.

remedy_recipe: Infuse 1 cup of dried thymus vulgaris flowers in 2 cups of boiling water for 5-7 minutes to create a strong tea.
Strain the tea and discard the solids then allow the liquid to cool down to room temperature.
Mix 1/2 cup of cooled tea with 2 tablespoons of beeswax and 2 tablespoons of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat and stir until the beeswax is fully melted and the mixture is smooth.
Pour the mixture into a clean container and allow it to cool and thicken for about 30 minutes.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh rosmarinus officinalis leaves and rinse them gently under cold running water for 30 seconds.
Combine 100g of dried rosmarinus officinalis leaves with 500ml of boiling distilled water and steep for 10 minutes.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth into a clean glass bowl, discarding the solids, and let it cool to room temperature within 60 minutes.
Mix 50g of beeswax, 20g of coconut oil, and 30ml of the cooled herbal infusion in a double boiler, heating until melted and smooth after 10 minutes.
Pour the warm cream mixture into sterilized glass jars and seal tightly, allowing it to cool and set at room temperature for 2 hours.

remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality cymbopogon citratus leaves in the early morning or evening, ensuring optimal essential oil content.
Dry the harvested leaves using a food dehydrator at 60°c for 24 hours or by air drying for 3-5 days.
Grind 20g of dried cymbopogon citratus leaves into a fine powder using a spice grinder and mix with 10g of coconut oil.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler at 40°c for 30 minutes, allowing the essential oils to infuse into the coconut oil.
Strain the infusion through cheesecloth or a coffee filter and transfer to sterilized containers, storing in the refrigerator for up to 6 months.

remedy_recipe: Harvest dried taraxacum officinale flowers in adequate amounts for the recipe.
Grind 30g of dried taraxacum officinale flowers into a fine powder using mortar and pestle.
Mix 10ml of carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil with 5g of powdered taraxacum officinale flowers.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 20 minutes to allow for infusion and solvent extraction.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth into a clean glass container, discarding the solids and storing it in an airtight container.

remedy_recipe: Grind 20 grams of dried panax ginseng root into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Combine the ground ginseng powder with 10 milliliters of sweet almond oil and 5 milliliters of coconut oil in a double boiler setup.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the oils have absorbed the medicinal properties of the ginseng.
Remove the mixture from heat and let it cool to room temperature before mixing in 2 teaspoons of beeswax and 1 teaspoon of vitamin e oil.
Pour the final mixture into a sterilized glass jar and store it in a cool, dark place for at least 24 hours before use.

Additionally, the sweet and comforting flavor of licorice cream can provide a sense of pleasure and satisfaction, encouraging individuals to eat and nourish their bodies once again.
remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: This compound has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce inflammation in the digestive tract and alleviate symptoms such as nausea and bloating, making it easier for individuals to eat.

Flavonoids: Licorice flavonoids, such as licochalcone A, have been shown to increase appetite by stimulating the production of ghrelin, a hormone that regulates hunger, and reducing the production of leptin, a hormone that suppresses appetite.
Saponins: Saponins in licorice root have been found to have antimicrobial properties, which can help to reduce gut dysbiosis (an imbalance of gut bacteria) that may contribute to loss of appetite.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the licorice plant are rich in glycyrrhizin, a compound that has anti-inflammatory properties and helps to stimulate appetite.
Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes of licorice contain compounds like liquiritin and isoliquiritigenin, which have been shown to improve digestion and increase appetite.
remedy_recipe: Collect and dry 20 grams of dried licorice root extract and grind it into fine powder.
Mix the powdered licorice root extract with 50 grams of beeswax and 30 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 10 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the beeswax is fully melted.
Remove the mixture from heat and let it cool to around 60 degrees celsius before whipping it with an electric whisk for 5 minutes.
Pour the whipped cream into sterile jars and store them in a cool place for at least 2 weeks before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Borage creams helps with loss of appetite because they contain the herb Calendula, which has been traditionally used to stimulate appetite and improve digestion. The cream's anti-inflammatory properties can also help soothe digestive issues such as bloating and nausea, making it easier for individuals to consume food. Additionally, borage's calming effects on the nervous system may reduce stress and anxiety-related loss of appetite, promoting a healthy and balanced diet.
remedy_properties: Gamma-Linolenic Acid (GLA): GLA has been shown to reduce inflammation in the body, which can help alleviate digestive issues and improve appetite.
Borago officinalis: The herb itself has been traditionally used to stimulate appetite and improve digestion, possibly due to its ability to soothe stomach irritation and reduce nausea.
Omega-6 fatty acids: Borage oil is rich in omega-6 fatty acids, which are important for maintaining the health of the gut lining and can help regulate hunger hormones.
remedy_parts: Roots: Borage roots are known for their calming effects on the digestive system, making them a popular choice for treating loss of appetite.
Seeds: Borage seeds contain healthy fats and omega-6 fatty acids, which can help stimulate appetite and improve overall nutritional absorption.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh borago officinalis leaves in abundance and rinse them thoroughly with cool water.
Combine one cup of the harvested leaves with two cups of cold-pressed olive oil in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a dark cabinet at room temperature for four to six weeks, allowing it to macerate slowly.
After the maceration period, strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another glass container, discarding the solids.
Mix one tablespoon of the herbal infused oil with three tablespoons of beeswax and heat gently over low heat until the wax melts and blends smoothly with the oil, creating a creamy consistency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold creams helps with loss of appetite because they contain a high concentration of flavonoids, which have been shown to stimulate appetite and improve digestion. The cream's anti-inflammatory properties also help to soothe the stomach and reduce nausea, making it easier for individuals to consume food. Additionally, pot marigold has been used traditionally to treat digestive issues such as constipation, which can contribute to a decrease in appetite. As a result, using pot marigold creams can help to restore a normal appetite and improve overall digestive health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Pot marigold cream's anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which can alleviate symptoms of appetite loss caused by conditions such as irritable bowel syndrome (IBS).

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidant compounds present in pot marigold creams can help protect the gut lining from damage and oxidative stress, which can improve digestion and absorption of nutrients, leading to increased appetite.

Sotheing and anti-irritant properties: Pot marigold cream's soothing and anti-irritant properties can help calm and protect the mucous membranes in the digestive tract, reducing irritation and discomfort that may contribute to loss of appetite.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots contain apigenin, an antioxidant and anti-inflammatory compound that helps stimulate appetite.
Seeds: The seeds are rich in omega-3 fatty acids, which have been shown to improve appetite and reduce inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh calendula officinalis flowers in the morning when they are at their peak potency.
Dry the harvested flowers using a food dehydrator or air drying method for 7-10 days.
Grind 20g of dried calendula flowers into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or spice grinder.
Mix 100ml of sweet almond oil with 5ml of beeswax and 2g of the ground calendula powder in a double boiler setup.
Heat the mixture for 15-20 minutes while stirring occasionally until the beeswax is fully melted and the cream has cooled to room temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow creams helps with loss of appetite because they soothe and calm the digestive system, alleviating discomfort and inflammation that can lead to a decrease in food intake. The anti-inflammatory properties of marshmallows also help reduce irritation in the mouth, throat, and esophagus, making eating less painful and more enjoyable. Additionally, marshmallow creams can aid in the digestion of food, allowing the body to absorb nutrients more effectively, which can further increase appetite and improve overall nutrition.
remedy_properties: Soothing Mucilage: The mucilages present in marshmallow creams coat the stomach lining, reducing inflammation and irritation, which can contribute to loss of appetite by making eating uncomfortable.

Demulcent Properties: Marshmallow creams' demulcent properties create a protective barrier on the mucous membranes, soothing digestive issues such as gastroesophageal reflux disease (GERD), heartburn, and inflammatory bowel disease (IBD), which can lead to loss of appetite.

Anti-Inflammatory Compounds: Marshmallow creams contain anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids, which help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, alleviating symptoms such as bloating, cramps, and abdominal pain that can contribute to loss of appetite.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the marshmallow plant contain mucilage, which helps to soothe and calm the digestive tract, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with loss of appetite.
Seeds: Marshmallow seeds have been traditionally used to treat digestive issues, including loss of appetite, as they are rich in fiber and omega-3 fatty acids that help regulate bowel movements.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30g of dried marshmallow root althaea officinalis and grind into fine powder.
Combine the ground marshmallow root with 100ml of warm distilled water in a heatproof cup.
Steep the mixture for 15 minutes, then strain through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another container.
Mix 30g of beeswax pellets, 20g of coconut oil and 10g of vitamin e oil with the herbal infusion.
Pour the mixture into a clean glass jar and let it cool to form a solid cream that can be stored for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valerian creams helps with loss of appetite because they contain the natural herb valerian root, which has a calming effect on the digestive system. The cream can soothe the stomach and intestines, reducing feelings of discomfort and indigestion that often lead to a decrease in appetite. By promoting relaxation and reducing inflammation, valerian creams can help regulate appetite and improve overall digestion, making it easier to eat and enjoy meals again. This natural approach can be especially helpful for individuals with stress-related eating disorders or those experiencing digestive issues.
remedy_properties: Sedative and calming effects: Valerian's sedative properties can help alleviate stress, anxiety, and restlessness, which are common underlying causes of loss of appetite.

Digestive soothing properties: Valerian has been traditionally used to calm digestive issues such as nausea, bloating, and indigestion, making it easier for individuals with reduced appetite to eat and digest food properly.

Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects: Valerian's antioxidant properties can help reduce inflammation in the body, which is often linked to loss of appetite due to chronic illness or injury, allowing the individual to recover and regain their appetite naturally.
remedy_parts: Roots: Valerian roots are a rich source of valerenic acid, a compound that helps to stimulate appetite and reduce feelings of nausea.
Rhyzomes: Valerian rhyzomes contain sesquiterpenes, which have been shown to have anti-anorectic properties, helping to increase food intake in individuals with loss of appetite.
remedy_recipe: Harvest valeriana officinalis roots in mid-summer when they are mature and dry them immediately.
Grind 250g of dried valeriana officinalis roots into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Mix 100g of the powdered valeriana officinalis with 200ml of cold-pressed sweet almond oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally, until it reaches a temperature of 60°c.
Allow the mixture to cool and solidify at room temperature before transferring it to a clean glass jar for storage.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for loss of appetite are topical preparations infused with herbs and other natural ingredients that help stimulate appetite and improve food digestion. They work by increasing blood flow to the stomach, reducing stress and anxiety, and enhancing the senses of taste and smell. Examples include ginger, peppermint, and triphala creams, which have been used to treat anorexia, nausea, and loss of appetite in individuals with cancer, HIV/AIDS, and other conditions, improving their overall quality of life and ability to eat and nourish themselves.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to loss of appetite that are treated with herbal creams are digestive issues such as constipation, irritable bowel syndrome (IBS), and inflammatory bowel disease (IBD). Herbal creams containing ingredients like aloe vera, slippery elm, and marshmallow root can help soothe and calm the digestive tract, reducing symptoms like bloating, cramps, and diarrhea. These creams may also contain anti-inflammatory compounds that reduce inflammation and promote healing in the gut.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal creams that help with loss of appetite is a blend of peppermint, ginger, and turmeric creams. Peppermint cream stimulates digestion and increases saliva production, while ginger cream reduces nausea and inflammation. Turmeric cream, rich in curcumin, has anti-inflammatory properties that promote appetite and digestion. When applied topically, these creams can help alleviate digestive issues, making it easier to regain a healthy appetite. This combination can be applied to the abdomen or wrists for optimal benefits.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by Avicenna journal of phytomedicine, traditional herbal remedies have been considered as complementary treatments for obesity. This preliminary study involved 50 participants, aged 18-65, who were randomly assigned to either an intervention group that received an Iranian Traditional Medicine (ITM)-based herbal candy, or a control group that received a placebo candy, for an 8-week period. The results showed that the herbal candy significantly reduced appetite and eating capacity indicators in the intervention group compared to the control group, with a mean decrease in hunger, satiety, and eating capacity indicators at all three intervals (30 min after herbal candy, 1 hour after meal, and 2 hours after meal) at both lunch and dinner meals.
